title: carex extensa f. pumila
intro: Carex extensa f. pumila, also known as dwarf sedge or small-fruited sedge, is a small, perennial plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with its roots and rhizomes exhibiting medicinal properties, particularly anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Carex extensa f. pumila include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being and reduce inflammation.
